summarization.a: The figure displays X-ray diffraction patterns of various lithium manganese oxide compounds (50cLi-MnO2, 10cLi-MnO2 and MnO2) at different cycles.
summarization.b: The figure shows X-ray diffraction patterns of lithium manganese oxide compounds (50cLi-MnO2, 10cLi-MnO2 and MnO2) and their lithiated phases.

a.question: What is the difference between the two XRD diffractograms shown in the figures?
a.answer: Both spectra show different planes of the samples. °2Θχ represents the in-plane and °2Θ represents the out-of-plane.

a.question: How does the intensity of the peaks change when the number of lithium cycles increases?
a.answer: As the number of cycles increases, lower peaks in quantity and intensity can be observed.

b.question: At which value and in which plane can an impurity phase be observed?
b.answer: An impurity phase can be observed in the out-of-plane direction at approximately 33°.

b.question: How many cycles are needed to produce the lithiated phase?
b.answer: At least 50 cycles are needed to produce this phase.
